steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what an energy ladder model advocate would say explains Brazil's shift away from solid fuels. The passage defines the model: fuel choice is driven by household income, with high-technology fuels displacing solid fuels as incomes rise.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'rising household incomes enabled the shift to high-technology fuels': directly applies the model's core mechanism. ✓
  • Choice B — 'greater fuel availability drove the shift': cites supply, not income — a different causal factor the model doesn't use. ✗
  • Choice C — 'relative pricing explained the change': pricing is outside the income-mediated framework the model describes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'income growth weakened the model's explanatory power': contradicts the model rather than supporting it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
